the Proposal. -

In order to provide relief to the debt-ridden farmers due to continuous drought and barrenness in the state, the Reference No. The Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Shetkari Samman Yojana-2017 has been implemented as per the decision of the government here. Benefit to 50.60 lakh eligible accounts and 44.04 lakh account holders eligible for benefits under the scheme.

The remaining 6.56 lakh eligible loan accounts are eligible for the benefit. Some farmers / cooperatives are eligible under this scheme and have not received the benefit. Various writ petitions have been filed in the High Court, Bombay Bench Aurangabad and Bench Nagpur. The petitioners concerned as well as other eligible persons in the said Rot Petitions.
To provide the benefits of the scheme to the account holders, Shri. The High Court has given orders to the government.
The remaining loan accounts that are eligible under the scheme, in accordance with the order passed by the Hon'ble High Court, will be

To pay the benefit of Rs. The proposal for grant of Supplementary Demands for funds to the tune of ₹1 crore was introduced in the December 2025 session. Accordingly, the fund sought by the department was Rs.
500.00 crores has been made available.

The Commissioner and Registrar of Co-operative Societies, Pune, vide Reference No. According to the letter of 3, the amount available under the Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Shetkari Samman Yojana, 2017 is Rs. A proposal to disburse the remaining amount of ₹1 crore to eligible creditors has been submitted and the proposal to disburse the funds was under consideration of the Government.
The Government's Decisions:

In the financial year 2025-26, the Programme Expenditure Scheme of the Department of Cooperation, Marketing and Textiles sanctioned by way of Supplementary Demand for Rs. The Government is approving the release of funds to the tune of ₹33 crore under the Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Shetkari Samman Yojana, 2017 (State Level) (2435 0082) for this purpose.

2. The Commissioner and Registrar of Cooperatives, Co-operative Societies, Pune vide Reference No. As per the proposal submitted on 3rd 06.03 .202E, the funds should be disbursed online to the eligible account holders of the banks and various executive cooperatives in the attached Form "A" and Form "B," following the prevailing rules and prescribed procedure. However, while allocating funds, the office of the Commissioner of Cooperation should ensure that the benefit is paid only to the loan accounts that are eligible under the green list.

3. The Registrar, Cooperative Societies, Subordinate Assistant Registrar (Forecasting & Planning), Cooperative Societies, Maharashtra State, Pune is being authorised as the Controlling Officer for expenditure of this provision. Also, the Accountant, Subordinate Cooperation Commissioner and Registrar, Cooperative Societies, Maharashtra State, Pune are being declared as Drawing and Disbursing Officers. The Commissioner and Registrar of Co-operative Societies, Maharashtra State, Pune should ensure that the funds are withdrawn and spent on time. It should also submit reports and expenditure reports to the government from time to time.

4. The said GA Demand No. V-02, 2435-Other Agricultural Programmes, 00, 60-Others, 101- Scheme for Waiver of Farmers, Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Shetkari Samman Yojana, 2017 (State Level) (2435 0082) -33 should be spent from the provision available for the year 2025-26 under the head Finance (Datum).
This Government decision is being issued as agreed under the Finance Department Informal Reference No. 206/2026 Expenditure-2, dated 17.03.2026.

The Government decision is available on the Government of Maharashtra website www-.maharashtra.gov.in with the computer code 202603311759071102. This government decision is being made by recording the evidence with a digital signature.
By order and in the name of the Governor of Maharashtra.
Earn Santosh OVERENT OF MAHARASTRA uD, ARUN PATIL
(Santosh Patil)
Additional Registrar & Joint Secretary, Government of Maharashtra
